title: Network Connection Failed
shortDescription: The TV cannot connect to the wireless network due to a Wi-Fi connectivity issue.
fullDescription:
Error 106 is one of the most common LG Smart TV errors, indicating the television is unable to establish a connection to the wireless network. This typically occurs when the TV detects the Wi-Fi network but fails to complete the connection handshake.

The error can be triggered by incorrect Wi-Fi passwords, router configuration issues, IP address conflicts, or problems with the TV's wireless module. In many cases, the issue is related to the router's security settings or channel congestion rather than a hardware fault in the TV itself.

LG recommends starting with basic troubleshooting such as power cycling both the TV and the router, and verifying the Wi-Fi password. If the issue persists, changing the router's wireless channel or updating the TV's firmware may resolve it.

causes.cause: IP address conflict on the network
Faulty Wi-Fi module in the TV

symptoms: TV displays Error 106 when trying to connect to Wi-Fi
Network settings show the Wi-Fi network but connection fails
Smart TV apps and streaming services are unavailable
TV intermittently drops Wi-Fi connection
Other devices connect to the same network without issues

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the TV and router
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV and your router from the power outlet. Wait 60 seconds. Plug the router back in first and wait for it to fully boot (all lights stable). Then plug the TV back in and turn it on.

fixSteps.title: Re-enter Wi-Fi credentials
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network > Wi-Fi Connection. Select your network and choose 'Forget Network' if available. Then reconnect by selecting the network again and carefully re-entering the password.

fixSteps.title: Set DNS manually
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network > Wi-Fi Connection > Advanced Settings. Change DNS from Automatic to Manual. Set DNS Server to 8.8.8.8 (Google DNS) or 1.1.1.1 (Cloudflare DNS). Press Connect.

fixSteps.title: Change router wireless channel
fixSteps.instruction: Log into your router's admin panel (usually 192.168.1.1 or 192.168.0.1). Navigate to Wireless Settings and change the channel from Auto to a specific channel (1, 6, or 11 for 2.4GHz). Save and restart the router.
tools: computer or phone for router access

fixSteps.title: Update TV firmware via USB
fixSteps.instruction: Visit lg.com/support, enter your TV model number, and download the latest firmware. Extract it to a USB drive. Insert the USB into the TV, go to Settings > General > About This TV > Check for Updates, and install from USB.

whenToCallPro: If the Wi-Fi module is confirmed faulty after trying all software troubleshooting steps, and the TV cannot connect to any wireless network.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV error 106 WiFi fix

faq.q: Why does my LG TV keep showing Error 106?
faq.a: Error 106 is usually caused by Wi-Fi connectivity issues. The most common fix is power cycling both the TV and router, then re-entering your Wi-Fi password. If it recurs, try using a wired Ethernet connection or changing your router's wireless channel.

faq.q: Can I use an Ethernet cable instead?
faq.a: Yes, connecting an Ethernet cable directly from your router to the TV bypasses Wi-Fi entirely and is the most reliable way to eliminate Error 106.

faq.q: Does Error 106 mean my TV's Wi-Fi is broken?
faq.a: Not necessarily. In most cases, it is a software or network configuration issue that can be resolved without hardware repair. A hardware fault in the Wi-Fi module is relatively rare.

title: DNS Resolution Failed
shortDescription: The TV is connected to the network but cannot resolve domain names due to a DNS server issue.
fullDescription:
Error 105 on LG Smart TVs indicates a DNS (Domain Name System) resolution failure. While the TV has successfully connected to the local network, it cannot translate website or service domain names into IP addresses, preventing access to streaming apps and online content.

This is almost always a network configuration issue rather than a TV hardware problem. The TV's default DNS settings may be pointing to an unresponsive DNS server, or the ISP's DNS servers may be experiencing temporary outages.

The most effective fix is manually changing the DNS server settings on the TV to a reliable public DNS service like Google DNS (8.8.8.8) or Cloudflare DNS (1.1.1.1). This bypasses the ISP's DNS servers entirely.

causes.cause: ISP DNS servers are down or slow
Incorrect DNS settings on the TV
Router not passing DNS settings correctly
Corrupted network settings on the TV

symptoms: Error 105 appears when opening apps or browser
TV is connected to Wi-Fi but cannot load content
Streaming apps show loading indefinitely
TV browser cannot open any websites
Network test shows connected but no internet

fixSteps.title: Set DNS to Google DNS
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network > Wi-Fi Connection. Select your connected network, then Advanced Settings. Change DNS Setting from Automatic to Manual. Enter 8.8.8.8 as the DNS server. Press Connect.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV from the wall outlet for 60 seconds. This clears the network cache. Plug it back in and check if the error is resolved.

fixSteps.title: Restart your router
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug your router and modem for 30 seconds. Plug the modem in first, wait 2 minutes, then plug in the router. Wait for all lights to stabilize before testing the TV.

fixSteps.title: Reset TV network settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network > Reset Network Settings (or Wi-Fi Connection > Forget All Networks). Then reconnect to your Wi-Fi network with the correct password and manually set DNS to 8.8.8.8.

fixSteps.title: Try a wired Ethernet connection
fixSteps.instruction: Connect an Ethernet cable from your router directly to the TV's LAN port. The TV should automatically detect the wired connection. If this works, the issue is specific to your Wi-Fi DNS configuration.

whenToCallPro: This error is almost always fixable without professional help. If changing DNS and resetting network settings does not resolve it, contact your ISP to verify their DNS service is operational.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV error 105 DNS fix

faq.q: What is DNS and why does it cause Error 105?
faq.a: DNS (Domain Name System) translates website names like netflix.com into IP addresses. When the DNS server your TV uses is not responding, the TV cannot find any online services, triggering Error 105.

faq.q: Will changing DNS affect my other devices?
faq.a: No, changing DNS on the TV only affects the TV. Your other devices will continue using their own DNS settings.

faq.q: Which DNS server should I use?
faq.a: Google DNS (8.8.8.8) and Cloudflare DNS (1.1.1.1) are the most reliable free public DNS servers. Either works well for LG TVs.

title: Server Connection Timeout
shortDescription: The TV cannot establish a connection to the content server or LG services.
fullDescription:
Error 137 indicates that the LG Smart TV is connected to the internet but cannot reach a specific content server or LG's own servers. This is often a temporary issue caused by server-side problems at LG or a streaming service, but can also be caused by local network issues.

This error frequently appears when trying to use the LG Content Store, LG Channels, or when the TV attempts to check for software updates. It can also occur when the TV's date and time settings are incorrect, as SSL certificates require accurate time to validate.

In most cases, the error resolves itself within a few hours when it is a server-side issue. If it persists, adjusting the TV's network settings or performing a software update can help.

causes.cause: LG server is temporarily down or overloaded
Incorrect date and time settings on the TV
ISP blocking certain ports or services

symptoms: Error 137 appears when opening LG Content Store
Cannot download or update apps
LG Channels fail to load
Firmware update check fails
Web browser shows connection error

fixSteps.title: Wait and retry
fixSteps.instruction: Error 137 is often a temporary server issue. Wait 30 minutes to 1 hour and try again. Check downdetector.com on your phone to see if LG services are experiencing widespread outages.

fixSteps.title: Verify date and time
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Time & Date. Enable 'Set Automatically' or manually set the correct date, time, and time zone. Incorrect time causes SSL certificate errors that trigger Error 137.

fixSteps.title: Change DNS settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network > Wi-Fi Connection > Advanced Settings. Set DNS manually to 8.8.8.8 or 1.1.1.1. This can help if your ISP's DNS is returning stale server addresses.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the TV completely
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the TV and unplug it from the wall. Press and hold the power button on the TV itself for 15 seconds to drain residual power. Wait 60 seconds, then plug back in and power on.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware via USB
fixSteps.instruction: If the TV cannot reach LG servers to update over the internet, download the latest firmware from lg.com/support on a computer. Copy it to a USB drive, insert into the TV, and go to Settings > General > About This TV > Update.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists for more than 24 hours and all troubleshooting steps fail, contact LG support as the issue may be related to your TV's registration with LG servers.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV error 137 fix server connection

faq.q: Is Error 137 a hardware issue?
faq.a: No, Error 137 is a software and network issue. It means the TV can reach the internet but cannot connect to a specific server. It does not indicate any hardware fault.

faq.q: Why does Error 137 happen at the same time every day?
faq.a: If Error 137 occurs at consistent times, it may be due to peak network congestion from your ISP or scheduled maintenance on LG servers. Try using a wired Ethernet connection during peak hours.

faq.q: Does a factory reset fix Error 137?
faq.a: A factory reset sometimes helps if the issue is caused by corrupted TV settings, but it is usually not necessary. Try the simpler DNS and time/date fixes first.

title: LG Server Communication Error
shortDescription: The TV cannot communicate with LG's servers for app downloads, updates, or account services.
fullDescription:
Error 201 on LG Smart TVs indicates a failure to communicate with LG's content and service servers. This error typically appears when attempting to access the LG Content Store, sign into an LG account, download apps, or perform system updates.

Unlike Error 137, which is often a temporary timeout, Error 201 tends to be more persistent and may indicate a deeper configuration issue. Common causes include incorrect region settings, an unregistered or blocked TV serial number, or problems with the LG ThinQ account.

The error can sometimes be resolved by re-signing into the LG account, verifying the TV's region settings match the actual location, or performing a factory reset as a last resort.

causes.cause: LG account sign-in session has expired
TV region settings do not match actual location
LG Content Store servers are down in your region
Corrupted LG account data on the TV
TV firmware is incompatible with current server API

symptoms: Error 201 when opening LG Content Store
Cannot sign into LG account on TV
App downloads fail with an error
LG ThinQ features do not work
Premium content or subscriptions not loading

fixSteps.title: Re-sign into LG account
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Account Management. Sign out of your LG account, wait 30 seconds, then sign back in with your credentials. This refreshes the authentication token.

fixSteps.title: Verify region settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Location or Settings > General > System > Location. Ensure the country/region is set to your actual location. Some LG Content Store features are region-locked.

fixSteps.title: Clear LG Content Store cache
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Storage or navigate to the LG Content Store app in Settings > Apps. Select the Content Store and choose 'Clear Cache' or 'Clear Data'. Restart the TV.

fixSteps.title: Update TV firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > About This TV > Check for Updates. Install any available firmware updates and restart the TV. Outdated firmware may not be compatible with LG's current servers.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset the TV
fixSteps.instruction: As a last resort, go to Settings > General > Reset to Initial Settings. The TV will restart and go through initial setup. You will need to re-enter Wi-Fi credentials and sign into all apps again.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after a factory reset, contact LG customer support directly as the issue may be related to your TV's server-side registration.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV error 201 content store fix

faq.q: Can I still use my TV if I get Error 201?
faq.a: Yes, the TV itself works fine for watching cable, antenna, and HDMI sources. Error 201 only affects LG-specific smart features like the Content Store and LG account services.

faq.q: Do I need an LG account to use my Smart TV?
faq.a: An LG account is needed to download apps from the Content Store, but most pre-installed apps like Netflix, YouTube, and Disney+ work without one.

title: Service Unavailable Error
shortDescription: The TV received a 503 Service Unavailable response from a streaming server or LG service.
fullDescription:
Error 503 on LG Smart TVs is an HTTP status code meaning 'Service Unavailable.' This error occurs when the server that the TV is trying to reach is temporarily overloaded, undergoing maintenance, or otherwise unable to handle the request.

This is almost always a server-side issue and not a problem with the TV itself. The error commonly appears when using the built-in web browser, accessing the LG Content Store during peak hours, or when a streaming service is experiencing high traffic.

The best approach is to wait and retry after a few minutes. If the error only appears on a specific app or service, that particular provider is likely experiencing issues.

causes.cause: Streaming service or LG server is overloaded
Too many simultaneous connections from your network
ISP throttling or blocking the service

symptoms: Error 503 message in the TV browser or app
Streaming app shows 'service unavailable'
LG Content Store fails to load
Error appears temporarily then resolves on its own
Only one specific app or service is affected

fixSteps.title: Wait and retry
fixSteps.instruction: Error 503 is typically temporary. Wait 5 to 15 minutes and try accessing the service again. Check downdetector.com on your phone to see if others are reporting the same issue.

fixSteps.title: Restart the app
fixSteps.instruction: Close the affected app completely by pressing the Home button, then long-pressing on the app card and selecting Close. Reopen the app from the home screen.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV for 60 seconds, then plug it back in. This clears the TV's temporary memory and network cache, which can help if the TV is caching a stale 503 response.

fixSteps.title: Check your internet connection
fixSteps.instruction: Run a network test on the TV: Settings > Network > Wired/Wi-Fi Connection > Advanced Settings > Connection Test. Verify your internet is working by testing another app or using a different device.

whenToCallPro: Error 503 does not require professional repair. If it persists for more than 24 hours on all services, contact your ISP to verify your internet connection.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV error 503 service unavailable fix

faq.q: Is Error 503 my TV's fault?
faq.a: No, Error 503 means the server you are trying to reach is temporarily unable to handle requests. It is a server-side issue, not a TV hardware or software problem.

faq.q: How long does Error 503 usually last?
faq.a: Most 503 errors resolve within minutes to a few hours. If a major streaming service is down, it typically recovers within 1-4 hours.

title: HDMI No Signal Detected
shortDescription: The TV displays 'No Signal' when an HDMI source is connected, indicating no video input is detected.
fullDescription:
The 'No Signal' message on LG TVs appears when the selected HDMI input is not receiving a valid video signal from the connected device. This is one of the most frequently reported issues with LG TVs and can affect any HDMI-connected device including cable boxes, gaming consoles, Blu-ray players, and streaming sticks.

The problem is usually caused by a loose HDMI cable connection, a faulty HDMI cable, the wrong input being selected, or a handshake failure between the TV and the source device. In some cases, the HDMI port on the TV or the connected device may be damaged.

LG TVs support HDMI-CEC (SimpLink) which can sometimes interfere with signal detection. Adjusting HDMI settings or disabling advanced HDMI features like Deep Color can also help resolve handshake issues.

causes.cause: Wrong input source selected on TV
Connected device is not outputting a signal

symptoms: TV shows 'No Signal' on screen
HDMI input appears blank or black
Audio works but no video (or vice versa)
Signal intermittently drops and reconnects
TV auto-switches away from the HDMI input

fixSteps.title: Check HDMI cable connections
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the HDMI cable from both the TV and the source device. Inspect both ends for damage or bent pins. Firmly reconnect the cable, ensuring it clicks into place on both ends.

fixSteps.title: Select the correct input
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Input or Source button on the LG remote. Select the HDMI port that your device is physically connected to (HDMI 1, HDMI 2, etc.). Make sure the source device is powered on.

fixSteps.title: Try a different HDMI cable
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the current HDMI cable with a known working cable. Use a High Speed HDMI cable or Ultra High Speed HDMI cable for 4K content. Cheap or very old cables can cause signal issues.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle both devices
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off both the TV and the source device. Unplug both from power for 30 seconds. Plug the TV in first and turn it on. Then plug in and turn on the source device. Select the correct HDMI input.

fixSteps.title: Disable HDMI Deep Color
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Devices > HDMI Settings > HDMI Deep Color. Toggle off Deep Color for the problematic HDMI port. Some older devices do not support HDMI Deep Color and this can cause signal issues.

fixSteps.title: Try a different HDMI port
fixSteps.instruction: Connect the device to a different HDMI port on the TV. If the device works on another port, the original port may be damaged. If it does not work on any port, the issue is likely with the source device or cable.

whenToCallPro: If the HDMI port is physically damaged (bent pins, loose connector) or if multiple HDMI ports have stopped working, the main board may need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV no signal HDMI fix

faq.q: Why does my LG TV say 'No Signal' when my cable box is on?
faq.a: First verify you have the correct HDMI input selected. Then check the cable connections. If using an A/V receiver, make sure it is set to the correct input as well. Power cycling all devices in the chain often fixes the handshake.

faq.q: Does 'No Signal' mean my HDMI port is broken?
faq.a: Not usually. A broken HDMI port is rare. The vast majority of 'No Signal' issues are caused by loose cables, wrong input selection, or HDMI handshake failures that can be fixed without repair.

faq.q: Which HDMI port should I use on my LG TV?
faq.a: For the best performance, use HDMI 2 (or the port labeled HDMI eARC) for your primary device. For gaming consoles on newer LG TVs, use the port that supports HDMI 2.1 for 4K 120Hz.

title: Screen of Death - TV Unresponsive
shortDescription: The TV screen goes completely black, red, or displays a frozen colored screen while the standby light remains on.
fullDescription:
The LG TV 'Screen of Death' refers to a condition where the television becomes completely unresponsive with a black, red, or solid-colored screen, even though the power indicator light shows the TV is on. The TV does not respond to the remote control or physical buttons.

This issue can be caused by a software crash in the webOS operating system, a failing power supply board, a defective T-Con (timing controller) board, or a failed LED backlight. On OLED models, it can indicate a panel driver failure.

A soft reset (unplugging for 60 seconds) resolves most software-related instances. If the issue recurs frequently, it typically indicates a hardware problem that requires board-level repair or replacement.
symptoms: Screen is completely black but standby light is on
Screen shows a solid red or colored screen
TV does not respond to remote or buttons
Audio works but no picture
Screen flashes briefly then goes black
TV repeatedly turns off and on

fixSteps.title: Perform a hard power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV from the wall outlet. Press and hold the power button on the TV itself (not the remote) for 30 seconds. Wait 60 seconds, then plug the TV back in. This forces a full system reset.

fixSteps.title: Try a different input source
fixSteps.instruction: If the TV shows any response, press the Input button and try switching to a different source (Live TV, HDMI 1, etc.). If you see the input menu, the display is working and the issue is likely the current source.

fixSteps.title: Check for backlight failure
fixSteps.instruction: In a dark room, shine a flashlight directly at the TV screen. If you can faintly see the picture, the backlight has failed but the screen itself works. This requires professional backlight replacement.

fixSteps.title: Check ventilation
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the TV has adequate ventilation. There should be at least 4 inches of clearance around all vents. Remove any objects blocking the vents on the back panel. Overheating can cause protective shutdown.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset from boot menu
fixSteps.instruction: If the TV briefly shows the LG logo then goes black, try pressing and holding the power button on the TV for 10-15 seconds during bootup. Some models enter a recovery mode that allows factory reset without accessing the settings menu.

whenToCallPro: If the hard power reset does not resolve the issue and the flashlight test reveals backlight failure, or if the screen of death recurs frequently, the TV needs board-level diagnosis and repair.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV screen of death black screen fix

faq.q: Is the Screen of Death fixable?
faq.a: Yes, in most cases. If it is a software crash, a simple power reset fixes it. If it is a hardware issue like a failed backlight or power supply, the TV can be repaired by replacing the affected board, which is usually cheaper than buying a new TV.

faq.q: Does the Screen of Death mean my TV is dead?
faq.a: No. If the standby light is on, the TV is receiving power. The issue is with the display system (backlight, T-Con, or software), not the TV as a whole.

faq.q: How much does it cost to fix a black screen on an LG TV?
faq.a: A backlight strip replacement costs $30-80 in parts. A power supply board costs $40-120. Professional labor adds $100-200. Total repair is typically $150-350, compared to $400-2000 for a new TV.

title: Netflix App Network Error
shortDescription: The Netflix app on the LG TV cannot connect to Netflix servers, displaying a network error code.
fullDescription:
Netflix errors on LG Smart TVs typically involve network connectivity codes such as NW-2-5 (network connectivity issue), NW-3-6 (DNS configuration problem), or UI-800-3 (stale cached data). These errors prevent the Netflix app from loading content or signing in.

The most common cause is that the Netflix app's cached data has become corrupted and needs to be cleared. Other causes include DNS resolution issues, network connectivity problems, or an outdated version of the Netflix app.

LG WebOS TVs from 2014 onward have Netflix built into the operating system. If the standard Netflix app repeatedly fails, updating the TV firmware often includes a Netflix app update as well.

causes.cause: TV firmware too old to support current Netflix API

symptoms: Netflix displays error code NW-2-5 or NW-3-6
Netflix shows UI-800-3 error
Netflix app loads but cannot play any content
Netflix gets stuck on loading screen
Netflix app crashes immediately after opening

fixSteps.title: Clear Netflix app data
fixSteps.instruction: From the LG Home screen, go to the Netflix app. Press and hold the OK/Enter button on the remote. Select 'Clear Cache' if available. Alternatively, go to Settings > Apps > Netflix > Clear Data. Restart the app.

fixSteps.title: Sign out and sign back in
fixSteps.instruction: Open Netflix on the TV. Go to the Netflix menu and select 'Sign Out' or 'Get Help > Sign Out.' Wait 30 seconds, then sign back into your Netflix account.

fixSteps.title: Change DNS settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to TV Settings > Network > Wi-Fi Connection > Advanced Settings. Set DNS manually to 8.8.8.8. This fixes NW-3-6 errors caused by DNS resolution problems.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV for 3 minutes (not just 60 seconds — Netflix recommends a longer wait). Plug it back in and try Netflix again.

fixSteps.title: Update TV firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > About This TV > Check for Updates. Install any available update. Firmware updates often include Netflix app updates for LG WebOS TVs.

whenToCallPro: Netflix errors on LG TVs do not require professional repair. If the Netflix app repeatedly fails after all steps, contact Netflix support directly or use an external streaming device like a Roku or Fire Stick.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV Netflix error NW-2-5 fix

faq.q: Why does Netflix not work on my LG TV but works on my phone?
faq.a: The Netflix app on LG TVs is a separate version from phone apps. It may have corrupted cache data or need an update. Clearing the app data and restarting usually fixes it.

faq.q: My LG TV is too old for Netflix — what can I do?
faq.a: LG TVs from before 2014 may no longer support Netflix. You can use an external streaming device like a Roku, Amazon Fire Stick, or Chromecast with Google TV, which plugs into an HDMI port.

faq.q: Why does Netflix keep buffering on my LG TV?
faq.a: Buffering is usually caused by slow internet. Netflix requires at least 5 Mbps for HD and 25 Mbps for 4K. Try a wired Ethernet connection for more consistent speeds.

title: YouTube App Error or Crash
shortDescription: The YouTube app on the LG TV crashes, fails to load, or displays a playback error.
fullDescription:
YouTube errors on LG Smart TVs can manifest as app crashes, playback failures, infinite loading screens, black screens within the app, or error messages about network connectivity. The YouTube app is one of the most used apps on LG WebOS TVs and issues are common after app or firmware updates.

The most frequent cause is a corrupted app cache, which accumulates over time and can cause performance issues. Memory management on LG WebOS is limited, and the YouTube app can consume significant resources, especially when playing 4K or HDR content.

Clearing the app cache, updating the app, or reinstalling it are the most effective fixes. In some cases, a TV firmware update resolves YouTube compatibility issues introduced by Google's server-side changes.

causes.cause: Insufficient TV memory (too many apps running)
WebOS version no longer supported by YouTube

symptoms: YouTube app crashes to home screen
YouTube shows playback error on videos
YouTube loads but shows black screen
YouTube search does not work
Videos buffer endlessly on YouTube
YouTube app is extremely slow

fixSteps.title: Clear YouTube app cache
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Home button. Navigate to the YouTube app icon on the launcher bar. Press and hold the OK button on the remote. Select 'Clear Cache' if the option appears. If not, go to Settings > Apps > YouTube > Clear Cache.

fixSteps.title: Reinstall YouTube app
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Home button. Navigate to the YouTube app. Press and hold OK, then select 'Uninstall' or 'Remove.' Go to the LG Content Store, search for YouTube, and reinstall it.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV from the wall for 60 seconds. This clears the TV's RAM and temporary files. Plug it back in and try YouTube again.

fixSteps.title: Close background apps
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Home button, then use the recent apps view. Close all running apps by hovering over each app card and pressing the X button. This frees up memory for YouTube.

fixSteps.title: Update TV firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > About This TV > Check for Updates. Install any available update. WebOS updates often include fixes for app compatibility.

whenToCallPro: YouTube app errors do not require professional repair. If the app continues to fail after reinstalling, consider using a Chromecast or other streaming device for YouTube.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV YouTube not working fix

faq.q: Why did YouTube stop working on my LG TV?
faq.a: Google periodically updates the YouTube app requirements. If your LG TV runs an older version of WebOS that is no longer supported, the YouTube app may stop working. Check for firmware updates, or use an external streaming device.

faq.q: Can I cast YouTube to my LG TV instead?
faq.a: Yes, you can cast YouTube from your phone to the TV using the cast button in the YouTube mobile app. The TV must be on the same Wi-Fi network as your phone.

title: Software Update Failure
shortDescription: The TV fails to download or install a firmware update, showing an update error message.
fullDescription:
LG Smart TVs periodically require firmware updates to fix bugs, improve performance, and maintain compatibility with streaming services. When an update fails, the TV may display an error message during the download or installation process.

Update failures can be caused by an unstable internet connection during the download, insufficient storage space on the TV, a corrupted download file, or a USB drive issue when updating manually. In rare cases, a power interruption during the update can leave the TV in a partially updated state.

If an over-the-air update fails repeatedly, updating via USB is the most reliable alternative. LG provides firmware downloads for all TV models on their support website.
symptoms: Update download progress stops or fails
Error message during firmware installation
TV restarts repeatedly during update
TV stuck on LG logo after failed update
Update notification keeps appearing despite failed attempts

fixSteps.title: Use a wired connection
fixSteps.instruction: Connect an Ethernet cable from your router directly to the TV. A wired connection is more stable for large firmware downloads. Then retry the update: Settings > General > About This TV > Check for Updates.

fixSteps.title: Free up storage space
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Storage. Delete unused apps to free space. You can also clear app caches. The TV needs adequate free space to download and install the update.

fixSteps.title: Update via USB drive
fixSteps.instruction: On a computer, go to lg.com/support and enter your TV model number. Download the latest firmware file. Format a USB drive as FAT32. Extract the firmware folder to the root of the USB drive. Insert the USB into the TV and go to Settings > General > About This TV > Check for Updates.

fixSteps.title: Perform a factory reset before updating
fixSteps.instruction: If updates consistently fail, try a factory reset first: Settings > General > Reset to Initial Settings. After the TV restarts and completes setup, immediately check for updates before installing apps.

fixSteps.title: Recovery mode for bricked TV
fixSteps.instruction: If the TV is stuck on the LG logo after a failed update, unplug it for 5 minutes. Plug it back in and immediately insert the USB with the firmware. Some LG TVs automatically detect the USB firmware in recovery mode and begin installation.

whenToCallPro: If the TV is stuck in a boot loop or will not start after a failed update, and USB recovery does not work, a professional can reflash the firmware using JTAG or replace the main board.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV firmware update failed fix USB

faq.q: Can a failed update brick my LG TV?
faq.a: It is rare but possible if power is lost during the installation phase. Most failed updates simply do not install and the TV continues running the old firmware. USB recovery can usually fix a TV stuck after a failed update.

faq.q: How do I find my LG TV model number?
faq.a: Go to Settings > General > About This TV. The model number is listed there. You can also find it on a sticker on the back of the TV.

faq.q: How often should I update my LG TV?
faq.a: LG releases firmware updates every few months. Enable automatic updates in Settings > General > About This TV > Auto Update to receive updates as they become available.

title: Bluetooth Connection or Pairing Failure
shortDescription: The TV fails to pair with or connect to a Bluetooth device such as headphones, soundbar, or keyboard.
fullDescription:
LG Smart TVs with WebOS support Bluetooth connectivity for headphones, speakers, soundbars, keyboards, and game controllers. When the Bluetooth connection fails, the TV may not detect nearby Bluetooth devices, fail during pairing, or drop the connection after pairing.

Common causes include Bluetooth being disabled in TV settings, the Bluetooth device being in the wrong pairing mode, too many paired devices stored in the TV, or interference from other wireless devices. Some LG TV models only support specific Bluetooth profiles (A2DP for audio, HID for input devices).

LG TVs can typically only connect to one Bluetooth audio device at a time. If a Bluetooth device was previously paired with another TV or phone, it may need to be reset to pairing mode before the LG TV can connect.

causes.cause: Bluetooth device not in pairing mode
Too many paired devices stored in TV memory

symptoms: TV does not detect Bluetooth device
Pairing starts but fails to complete
Bluetooth device connects then immediately disconnects
Audio plays from TV speakers instead of Bluetooth device
Bluetooth menu is grayed out
Paired device shows as disconnected

fixSteps.title: Enable Bluetooth on TV
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Sound > Sound Out > Bluetooth Device (or Settings > General > External Devices > Bluetooth). Make sure Bluetooth is turned on.

fixSteps.title: Put device in pairing mode
fixSteps.instruction: On your Bluetooth device, activate pairing mode (usually by holding the power or Bluetooth button for 5-10 seconds until the LED flashes rapidly). The device must be in pairing mode, not just powered on.

fixSteps.title: Remove old paired devices
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Sound > Sound Out > Bluetooth Device. If you see previously paired devices listed, remove or forget them. LG TVs have a limit on stored Bluetooth pairings.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle both devices
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the Bluetooth device and the TV. Unplug the TV for 60 seconds. Turn on the TV first, then put the Bluetooth device into pairing mode and try connecting again.

fixSteps.title: Reduce wireless interference
fixSteps.instruction: Move other wireless devices (Wi-Fi routers, microwaves, baby monitors) away from the TV and Bluetooth device. Keep the Bluetooth device within 10 feet of the TV during initial pairing.

whenToCallPro: If the Bluetooth option is completely missing from the TV settings menu or if no Bluetooth devices can ever be detected, the Bluetooth module may be faulty and need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV Bluetooth not working pairing fix

faq.q: Can I connect two Bluetooth headphones to my LG TV at the same time?
faq.a: Most LG TVs only support one Bluetooth audio device at a time. Some newer models with WebOS 6.0+ support dual Bluetooth audio output, but this is not available on all models.

faq.q: Why is there audio delay with Bluetooth on my LG TV?
faq.a: Bluetooth audio has inherent latency. LG TVs with aptX or aptX Low Latency codec support have less delay. You can adjust audio sync in Settings > Sound > AV Sync Adjustment to compensate.

faq.q: Does my LG TV support Bluetooth?
faq.a: Most LG Smart TVs from 2017 onward include Bluetooth. Check your model specifications on lg.com or look for the Bluetooth option in Settings > Sound > Sound Out.

title: No Sound or Audio Output Error
shortDescription: The TV displays a picture but produces no sound from internal speakers or connected audio devices.
fullDescription:
Loss of audio on LG TVs is a common issue that can affect the internal speakers, HDMI ARC/eARC output, optical output, or Bluetooth audio. The TV may show a picture normally but produce no sound at all, or sound may cut out intermittently.

The most common causes are incorrect sound output settings (audio routed to an external device that is not connected), muted volume, HDMI-CEC (SimpLink) conflicts, or a software glitch. On rare occasions, the internal speakers or audio amplifier on the main board may have failed.

LG TVs have multiple audio output options, and the sound output setting must match the actual audio setup. For example, if 'Sound Out' is set to 'HDMI ARC' but no soundbar is connected, the internal speakers will be silent.

causes.cause: Sound output set to external device that is not connected
TV or source volume is muted or at minimum

symptoms: Picture displays normally but no sound
Sound works on some inputs but not others
Volume bar shows on screen but no audio heard
Sound cuts out intermittently
Sound only comes from TV speakers, not soundbar
Distorted or crackling audio

fixSteps.title: Check sound output settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Sound > Sound Out. Make sure it is set to 'TV Speaker' if using internal speakers, or 'HDMI ARC' if using a soundbar connected via ARC/eARC. This is the most common cause of no sound.

fixSteps.title: Unmute and check volume
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Mute button on the remote to make sure the TV is not muted. Increase the volume. Also check if the source device (cable box, game console) has its own volume control and is not muted.

fixSteps.title: Change audio format
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Sound > Additional Settings > Digital Sound Out. Change from 'Auto' or 'Pass Through' to 'PCM.' Some content sends audio formats that the TV speakers cannot decode.

fixSteps.title: Disable SimpLink/CEC
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Devices > HDMI Settings > SimpLink (HDMI-CEC). Turn it off. SimpLink can automatically redirect audio to a connected device. Restart the TV after disabling.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV from power for 60 seconds, then plug it back in. This resets the audio system and often resolves software-related sound issues.

whenToCallPro: If the internal speakers produce no sound after verifying all settings and performing a power reset, the speakers or the audio amplifier circuit on the main board may have failed.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV no sound fix audio

faq.q: Why does my LG TV have picture but no sound?
faq.a: The most common cause is the Sound Out setting being set to an external device (like HDMI ARC) when no external speaker is connected. Go to Settings > Sound > Sound Out and select 'TV Speaker.'

faq.q: Why does sound only work on some channels?
faq.a: Some channels broadcast in surround sound formats that the TV speakers cannot decode. Go to Settings > Sound > Additional Settings > Digital Sound Out and set it to PCM to convert all audio to stereo.

title: HDMI ARC/eARC Audio Not Working
shortDescription: No audio is transmitted from the TV to the soundbar or AV receiver through the HDMI ARC or eARC connection.
fullDescription:
HDMI ARC (Audio Return Channel) and eARC (Enhanced Audio Return Channel) allow the TV to send audio to a soundbar or AV receiver through a single HDMI cable. When this feature stops working, the soundbar receives no audio from the TV despite being connected.

ARC issues are among the most common LG TV problems and are typically caused by incorrect settings, HDMI-CEC being disabled, using the wrong HDMI port, or an incompatible audio format. LG TVs designate one specific HDMI port for ARC (usually HDMI 2, labeled 'eARC/ARC').

The TV must have Sound Out set to HDMI ARC and SimpLink (HDMI-CEC) enabled for ARC to function. Additionally, the audio format must be compatible with the receiving device — setting Digital Sound Out to 'PCM' guarantees compatibility with all soundbars.

causes.cause: Sound Out not set to HDMI ARC
Wrong HDMI port used (not the ARC port)
Audio format not supported by soundbar

symptoms: Soundbar connected via ARC produces no sound
TV speakers play sound instead of soundbar
Sound cuts in and out through ARC
Soundbar shows connected but no audio
Dolby Atmos content has no audio through ARC

fixSteps.title: Use the correct HDMI port
fixSteps.instruction: Connect the soundbar to the HDMI port labeled 'ARC' or 'eARC/ARC' on the TV. This is typically HDMI 2 on LG TVs. ARC only works on this specific port.

fixSteps.title: Set Sound Out to HDMI ARC
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Sound > Sound Out. Select 'HDMI ARC.' If using eARC, also go to Settings > Sound > Additional Settings and enable 'eARC Support.'

fixSteps.title: Enable SimpLink/HDMI-CEC
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Devices > HDMI Settings > SimpLink (HDMI-CEC). Turn it ON. ARC requires CEC to be enabled to communicate with the soundbar.

fixSteps.title: Set audio format to PCM
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Sound > Additional Settings > Digital Sound Out. Change from 'Auto' or 'Pass Through' to 'PCM.' This ensures the audio is in a format compatible with all soundbars.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle all devices
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug both the TV and the soundbar. Wait 60 seconds. Plug in and turn on the TV first, then the soundbar. Select HDMI ARC sound output again.

fixSteps.title: Try a different HDMI cable
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the HDMI cable with a High Speed HDMI cable or Ultra High Speed HDMI cable. For eARC, you specifically need an Ultra High Speed HDMI cable (HDMI 2.1 rated).

partsNeeded.part: Ultra High Speed HDMI Cable (for eARC)

whenToCallPro: If HDMI ARC audio does not work after verifying all settings, cables, and ports, the ARC functionality on the TV's main board may be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV HDMI ARC no sound soundbar fix

faq.q: What is the difference between ARC and eARC?
faq.a: ARC supports compressed audio formats like Dolby Digital. eARC supports uncompressed and high-bitrate formats like Dolby Atmos and DTS:X. eARC requires an Ultra High Speed HDMI cable and both devices must support eARC.

faq.q: Do I need to enable CEC for ARC to work?
faq.a: Yes, HDMI ARC requires HDMI-CEC (SimpLink on LG TVs) to be enabled. CEC allows the TV and soundbar to communicate over the HDMI connection.

faq.q: Can I use optical instead of ARC?
faq.a: Yes, if ARC is not working, you can connect an optical (Toslink) cable from the TV's optical output to the soundbar as a workaround. However, optical does not support Dolby Atmos.

title: Remote Control Not Responding
shortDescription: The LG Magic Remote or standard IR remote does not control the TV, or the pointer cursor does not appear.
fullDescription:
LG Smart TVs use two types of remotes: the standard IR (infrared) remote and the Magic Remote with pointer functionality and Bluetooth connectivity. When the remote stops working, the cause depends on which type is being used.

For IR remotes, the issue is usually dead batteries, an obstructed IR sensor, or the remote itself being faulty. For the Magic Remote, the problem often involves Bluetooth pairing — the remote needs to be re-paired with the TV. The Magic Remote uses both Bluetooth (for the pointer and voice commands) and IR (for basic power and volume).

If the Magic Remote's pointer (cursor) disappears but buttons still work for basic functions, the Bluetooth connection has been lost and the remote needs to be re-registered with the TV.
symptoms: TV does not respond to any remote button presses
Magic Remote pointer cursor does not appear
Remote works intermittently or from very close range only
Voice commands on Magic Remote do not work
TV responds to physical buttons but not to the remote

fixSteps.title: Replace batteries
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the battery cover on the back of the remote. Replace both AA batteries with fresh ones. Ensure they are inserted in the correct orientation (+/-). Test the remote.

fixSteps.title: Re-register the Magic Remote
fixSteps.instruction: Point the Magic Remote at the TV and press and hold the OK (wheel) button and the Back button simultaneously for 5 seconds. The remote LED will flash. Point it at the TV and press the OK button to complete registration.

fixSteps.title: Check for IR interference
fixSteps.instruction: Remove any objects between the remote and the TV. Other IR devices (fireplaces, LED lights, sound bars) can interfere with the IR sensor. Try pressing buttons while pointing the remote directly at the TV's IR receiver (usually at the bottom center).

fixSteps.title: Test remote with phone camera
fixSteps.instruction: Open your phone's camera app. Point the remote at the phone camera and press any button. If the remote is working, you will see a purple/white flash from the IR LED through the camera (invisible to the naked eye). If no light appears, the remote may be faulty.

fixSteps.title: Unpair and re-pair Magic Remote
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Devices > External Devices > Magic Remote Settings (use the TV's physical buttons or the LG ThinQ phone app to navigate). Select 'Unpair' or 'Deregister.' Then re-register the remote by pressing Home + Back buttons for 5 seconds.

whenToCallPro: If the remote is confirmed working (IR test passes, batteries are fresh) but the TV still does not respond, the TV's IR sensor or Bluetooth receiver may need repair.
videoSearchQuery: LG Magic Remote not working pairing fix

faq.q: Can I use the LG ThinQ app instead of the remote?
faq.a: Yes, download the LG ThinQ app on your smartphone. Connect it to the same Wi-Fi network as the TV. The app functions as a full remote control replacement including pointer functionality.

faq.q: Why does my Magic Remote pointer drift or shake?
faq.a: The Magic Remote uses a gyroscope for the pointer. If it drifts, try placing the remote on a flat surface for 10 seconds to recalibrate. If it persists, re-register the remote.

faq.q: Is the Magic Remote compatible with all LG TVs?
faq.a: No, the Magic Remote requires an LG Smart TV with WebOS. Different remote models are compatible with different TV years. Check the remote model number against your TV model on lg.com.

title: SimpLink (HDMI-CEC) Communication Failure
shortDescription: The TV's SimpLink (HDMI-CEC) feature does not control connected devices or causes unexpected behavior.
fullDescription:
SimpLink is LG's implementation of HDMI-CEC (Consumer Electronics Control), which allows the TV remote to control connected HDMI devices and enables features like automatic input switching, one-touch play, and system power off. When SimpLink malfunctions, connected devices may not respond to the TV remote, the TV may switch inputs unexpectedly, or devices may not power on/off together.

HDMI-CEC compatibility issues are common because different manufacturers implement CEC slightly differently. A device that works with Samsung's Anynet+ may not work perfectly with LG's SimpLink. Multiple CEC devices on the same TV can also conflict with each other.

SimpLink issues are almost always configuration-related and can be resolved by adjusting settings or re-establishing the CEC connection through a power cycle.

causes.cause: CEC conflict between multiple HDMI devices
Device CEC implementation incompatible with SimpLink

symptoms: TV remote does not control connected devices
TV switches HDMI inputs randomly
Connected device does not turn on/off with TV
Sound output switches unexpectedly
SimpLink menu shows no connected devices
TV turns on by itself when a device is used

fixSteps.title: Enable SimpLink
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Devices > HDMI Settings > SimpLink (HDMI-CEC). Make sure it is turned ON. Also enable 'Auto Power Sync' if you want devices to turn on/off with the TV.

fixSteps.title: Enable CEC on connected devices
fixSteps.instruction: On your soundbar, Blu-ray player, or game console, go to its settings and enable CEC/HDMI Control. Each brand has a different name: Samsung (Anynet+), Sony (Bravia Sync), Roku (1-Touch Play), etc.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle all HDMI devices
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug the TV and ALL connected HDMI devices. Wait 60 seconds. Plug in and turn on the TV first, then turn on each connected device one at a time. This re-establishes CEC handshakes.

fixSteps.title: Reduce CEC devices
fixSteps.instruction: If you have multiple CEC-enabled devices causing conflicts, try disabling CEC on devices that do not need it. Only keep CEC enabled on the one or two devices you want to control with the TV remote.

fixSteps.title: Try a different HDMI cable
fixSteps.instruction: Some cheap HDMI cables do not pass the CEC signal properly. Replace with a certified High Speed or Ultra High Speed HDMI cable. CEC uses a dedicated pin (pin 13) in the HDMI connector.

whenToCallPro: SimpLink issues rarely require professional repair. If the feature is enabled but no devices are ever detected on any HDMI port, the CEC controller on the main board may be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV SimpLink CEC not working fix

faq.q: What devices work with LG SimpLink?
faq.a: SimpLink is compatible with most HDMI-CEC devices including soundbars, Blu-ray players, game consoles, streaming devices, and AV receivers. However, some devices may have limited compatibility due to different CEC implementations.

faq.q: Can I turn off SimpLink for just one device?
faq.a: You cannot selectively disable SimpLink per port on the TV side. Instead, disable CEC in the settings of the specific device you do not want controlled by SimpLink.

faq.q: Why does my TV turn on when I turn on my PS5?
faq.a: This is HDMI-CEC working as designed. Your PS5 sends a CEC 'one-touch play' signal that turns on the TV. To stop this, either disable SimpLink on the TV or disable HDMI Device Link on the PS5.

title: Frequent Wi-Fi Disconnection
shortDescription: The TV repeatedly loses its Wi-Fi connection, requiring reconnection to the network.
fullDescription:
Frequent Wi-Fi disconnections on LG Smart TVs cause streaming interruptions, app failures, and a frustrating user experience. Unlike a complete Wi-Fi failure, this issue involves the TV connecting successfully but then losing the connection after minutes or hours of use.

This problem is often caused by the TV's power management settings putting the Wi-Fi module to sleep, interference from nearby devices on the same Wi-Fi channel, the TV being too far from the router, or router-side settings like automatic channel switching or band steering that confuse the TV.

LG TVs are known to have more Wi-Fi stability issues on the 5GHz band at longer ranges. If possible, use the 2.4GHz band for greater range or a wired Ethernet connection for the most reliable experience.

causes.cause: Wi-Fi signal too weak (TV too far from router)
TV power management disabling Wi-Fi module
Router band steering causing band hopping
TV firmware bug causing Wi-Fi drops

symptoms: Streaming stops with 'no internet connection' message
TV shows connected to Wi-Fi but no internet intermittently
Wi-Fi disconnects when TV wakes from standby
Must manually reconnect to Wi-Fi frequently
Other devices on same network work fine

fixSteps.title: Disable Quick Start+
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Quick Start+ and turn it OFF. This feature puts the TV in a low-power state that can cause the Wi-Fi module to disconnect. With it off, the TV fully powers down and reconnects Wi-Fi on startup.

fixSteps.title: Set a static IP address
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network > Wi-Fi Connection > Advanced Settings. Switch from DHCP to Manual. Set a static IP (e.g., 192.168.1.100), subnet mask (255.255.255.0), gateway (your router IP), and DNS (8.8.8.8). This prevents IP conflicts that cause disconnections.

fixSteps.title: Switch to 2.4GHz Wi-Fi band
fixSteps.instruction: If your router broadcasts separate 2.4GHz and 5GHz networks, connect the TV to the 2.4GHz network. 2.4GHz has better range and wall penetration, which is more reliable for TVs that are far from the router.

fixSteps.title: Set fixed router channel
fixSteps.instruction: Log into your router admin panel. Set the Wi-Fi channel to a fixed channel (1, 6, or 11 for 2.4GHz) instead of Auto. Auto channel switching can cause the TV to momentarily disconnect when the router switches channels.

fixSteps.title: Use a wired Ethernet connection
fixSteps.instruction: For the most reliable connection, run an Ethernet cable from your router to the TV's LAN port. The TV will automatically prefer the wired connection over Wi-Fi. If running a cable is impractical, consider a powerline Ethernet adapter.

whenToCallPro: If the TV Wi-Fi module is confirmed faulty (drops connection even next to the router with a strong signal), it may need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV WiFi keeps disconnecting fix

faq.q: Why does my LG TV lose Wi-Fi but my phone does not?
faq.a: LG TVs have less powerful Wi-Fi antennas than phones and may struggle with weak signals. The TV's power management also aggressively disables Wi-Fi to save energy. Disabling Quick Start+ and moving the router closer can help.

faq.q: Should I use 2.4GHz or 5GHz for my LG TV?
faq.a: Use 5GHz if the TV is close to the router (same room) for faster speeds. Use 2.4GHz if the TV is farther away or separated by walls, as it has better range.

title: WebOS Operating System Crash or Freeze
shortDescription: The TV's WebOS operating system crashes, freezes, or becomes unresponsive during use.
fullDescription:
LG Smart TVs run WebOS as their operating system. When WebOS crashes or freezes, the TV may become completely unresponsive, display a static image, show a black screen, or continuously restart in a loop. This is different from individual app crashes, as the entire operating system becomes non-functional.

WebOS crashes are typically caused by memory leaks from apps, corrupted system files after a failed update, insufficient storage space, or conflicts between apps. Older LG TVs with less RAM are more prone to WebOS crashes, especially when running modern streaming apps that require more memory.

A simple power cycle resolves most WebOS crashes. Persistent crashes may require clearing app data, performing a factory reset, or updating the firmware.

causes.cause: Memory overload from running too many apps
Corrupted system files from failed update
App conflict or incompatible app version

symptoms: TV screen freezes and does not respond to input
TV restarts on its own repeatedly
WebOS home screen takes a very long time to load
Apps crash immediately after opening
TV gets stuck on the LG logo during boot
Cursor and animations become extremely laggy

fixSteps.title: Force restart the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the power button on the remote for 10 seconds until the TV turns off and restarts. If the remote does not work, unplug the TV from power, wait 60 seconds, and plug it back in.

fixSteps.title: Close all background apps
fixSteps.instruction: After the TV restarts, press the Home button. Hover over each app in the recent apps bar and press the X to close it. This frees up RAM for the system to run smoothly.

fixSteps.title: Free up storage space
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Storage. Delete apps you do not use. Clear cache for apps that have grown large. WebOS needs free storage space for swap memory and temporary files.

fixSteps.title: Update WebOS firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > About This TV > Check for Updates. Install any available firmware update. If the TV crashes during the check, use a USB drive to install the update manually.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: If crashes persist, go to Settings > General > Reset to Initial Settings. This erases all apps, settings, and accounts, returning the TV to its original state. You will need to set up the TV again from scratch.

whenToCallPro: If WebOS continues to crash after a factory reset and firmware update, the main board's memory or processor may be failing, requiring hardware replacement.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV WebOS crash freeze fix

faq.q: Will a factory reset delete my apps?
faq.a: Yes, a factory reset removes all installed apps, account logins, and custom settings. Pre-installed apps like Netflix and YouTube will be restored automatically, but you will need to re-download any additional apps from the Content Store.

faq.q: Why does my LG TV get slower over time?
faq.a: WebOS accumulates cache data and background processes over time. Periodically restarting the TV (unplugging for 60 seconds), closing unused apps, and clearing app caches can help maintain performance.

title: Content Store Region Error
shortDescription: The LG Content Store cannot load because of a region or location verification issue.
fullDescription:
Error 023 appears when the LG Content Store cannot verify the TV's region or detects a mismatch between the configured location and the actual network location. This can happen when the TV's region settings do not match the country where it is being used, when using a VPN, or after purchasing a TV from a different region.

The LG Content Store is region-locked, meaning different apps and content are available depending on the TV's configured country. If the region is set incorrectly, the Content Store may refuse to load entirely.

To resolve this, verify the TV's location settings match your actual country. If you purchased an imported TV, you may need to change the region settings to your current country, though this may change which apps are available.

causes.cause: TV region set to a different country
VPN or proxy interfering with location detection
TV imported from a different region

symptoms: Error 023 when opening Content Store
Content Store shows different country's apps
Cannot download apps from Content Store
Content Store loads but shows limited selection

fixSteps.title: Check region settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > System > Location or Settings > General > Location. Set the country/region to your actual current location. The TV may restart after changing this setting.

fixSteps.title: Disable any VPN
fixSteps.instruction: If you are using a VPN on your router, temporarily disable it. LG Content Store uses your IP address to verify your location. A VPN can make it appear you are in a different country.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle and retry
fixSteps.instruction: After changing region settings, unplug the TV for 60 seconds. Plug it back in and open the Content Store. The TV needs to re-register with LG servers using the new region setting.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset with correct region
fixSteps.instruction: If the region cannot be changed in settings, perform a factory reset: Settings > General > Reset to Initial Settings. During the initial setup, select the correct country/region.

whenToCallPro: This error does not require professional repair. If changing the region setting does not work after a factory reset, contact LG support for assistance with your TV's regional configuration.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV error 023 content store region fix

faq.q: Will changing my TV's region affect my apps?
faq.a: Yes, the LG Content Store offers different apps depending on the region. Some apps may become unavailable after changing the region, while others may become available.

faq.q: Can I use a TV bought in another country?
faq.a: Yes, but you may need to change the region settings to your current country. Some features like over-the-air TV tuner frequencies may differ between regions.

title: Power/Standby LED Blinking Pattern
shortDescription: The TV's power indicator LED blinks in a pattern, indicating a hardware diagnostic code.
fullDescription:
When an LG TV fails to start and the standby/power LED blinks in a specific pattern, the TV is communicating a diagnostic error code through the blink sequence. Different patterns indicate different hardware failures, similar to error beep codes on computers.

Common blink patterns include: 2 blinks (backlight failure), 3 blinks (power supply issue), 4 blinks (main board failure), and 6 blinks (T-Con board failure). The exact meaning can vary by TV model and year.

Blink codes indicate hardware-level failures that typically require component replacement. However, before assuming a hardware failure, a hard power reset should always be attempted first, as power surges or software glitches can sometimes trigger false blink codes.
symptoms: Power LED blinks repeatedly in a pattern then TV shuts off
TV tries to turn on, shows LG logo briefly, then LED blinks
TV clicks or makes relay sounds when trying to power on
LED blinks a set number of times, pauses, then repeats
TV screen remains completely black while LED blinks

fixSteps.title: Count the blink pattern
fixSteps.instruction: Power on the TV and carefully count the number of times the LED blinks before pausing. Write down the pattern (e.g., 3 blinks, pause, 3 blinks). This identifies which component has failed.

fixSteps.title: Perform a hard power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV from the wall. Press and hold the power button on the TV (not the remote) for 30 seconds. Wait 5 full minutes. Plug the TV back in and try to power it on. This can clear false blink codes caused by power surges.

fixSteps.title: Check the backlight (2 blinks)
fixSteps.instruction: If you see 2 blinks, the backlight may have failed. In a dark room, turn on the TV and shine a flashlight at the screen. If you see a faint image, the backlight strips need replacement. This is a common failure on LG LED TVs.

fixSteps.title: Inspect power supply board
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV. Remove the back panel screws and carefully remove the back cover. Visually inspect the power supply board for bulging or leaking capacitors, burn marks, or damaged components. Do not touch any components while the TV is plugged in.

fixSteps.title: Replace the failed component
fixSteps.instruction: Based on the blink code diagnosis, order the appropriate replacement board (power supply, T-Con, or main board). Disconnect the faulty board's ribbon cables and power connectors, remove it, and install the replacement. Reassemble and test.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable opening the TV and working with electronics, or if the blink code indicates a main board failure. Backlight strip replacement on larger TVs requires careful panel handling.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV blinking light code repair

faq.q: What do different blink codes mean on LG TVs?
faq.a: Common patterns: 2 blinks = backlight failure, 3 blinks = power supply issue, 4 blinks = main board fault, 6 blinks = T-Con board problem. Exact codes vary by model — search your specific model number for accurate interpretation.

faq.q: Is it worth repairing a TV with a blink code?
faq.a: Usually yes. A backlight strip set costs $25-80 and a power supply board costs $30-100. Even with professional labor, the repair is typically $150-350, compared to $400-2000 for a new TV of similar size and quality.

faq.q: Can I repair the boards myself?
faq.a: Board replacement is within reach for anyone comfortable with basic electronics. The boards are modular — they plug in with connectors and are held by screws. However, backlight repair requires carefully lifting the LCD panel, which can be damaged if mishandled.

title: OLED Pixel Refresher Cycle
shortDescription: The OLED TV is running its pixel refresher maintenance cycle, which takes up to one hour to complete.
fullDescription:
LG OLED TVs have a built-in pixel refresher feature that runs automatically to prevent and reduce image retention (temporary burn-in). There are two types: a short refresher that runs after every 4 hours of cumulative use (takes a few minutes), and a long refresher that runs after every 2000 hours (takes about an hour).

During the pixel refresher cycle, the TV screen appears off but the standby light may blink or glow differently. The TV should not be unplugged or interrupted during this process. If interrupted, the refresher will attempt to run again the next time the TV is turned off.

The pixel refresher is a normal maintenance function and not an error. However, if the TV seems to run it excessively or if image retention persists after the cycle completes, there may be a panel issue requiring attention.

causes.cause: Normal automatic maintenance after 4 hours of use
Panel aging requiring more frequent refreshing

symptoms: TV screen appears off after turning TV off but standby light is different
TV takes longer than usual to turn off
Notification about pixel refresher on screen
Cannot turn TV on during the cycle
Progress bar shown briefly before TV screen goes dark

fixSteps.title: Wait for the cycle to complete
fixSteps.instruction: Do not unplug the TV. The short pixel refresher takes 5-10 minutes and runs automatically when you turn the TV off. The long refresher takes about 1 hour. Let it finish naturally.

fixSteps.title: Manually run pixel refresher
fixSteps.instruction: If you notice image retention, go to Settings > General > OLED Panel Care > Pixel Refresher. Select 'Start Now.' The TV will turn off and run the cycle. Do not use the TV until it completes.

fixSteps.title: Enable screen shift and logo dimming
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > OLED Panel Care. Enable 'Screen Shift' (slightly moves the image to prevent burn-in) and 'Logo Luminance Adjustment' (dims static logos). These features reduce the need for pixel refreshing.

fixSteps.title: Reduce static content display
fixSteps.instruction: Avoid displaying static images, news tickers, or game HUDs for extended periods. Use the TV's screen saver feature and avoid maximum brightness settings to reduce the stress on OLED pixels.

whenToCallPro: If image retention persists after running the manual pixel refresher multiple times, the OLED panel may be experiencing permanent burn-in. Contact LG support about warranty coverage for panel replacement.
videoSearchQuery: LG OLED TV pixel refresher burn-in fix

faq.q: Is it safe to unplug the TV during pixel refresher?
faq.a: You should avoid unplugging the TV during the pixel refresher cycle. While it will not damage the TV, the interrupted cycle will need to run again later, and incomplete cycles may leave image retention uncorrected.

faq.q: How do I know if pixel refresher is running?
faq.a: When the pixel refresher is active, the TV screen is off but the power indicator light may behave differently (pulse slowly or stay a different color). The TV may also feel warm despite the screen being off.

faq.q: Does pixel refresher reduce OLED lifespan?
faq.a: The pixel refresher slightly wears the OLED pixels each time it runs, which is why LG limits automatic cycles. Do not run the manual pixel refresher more than necessary. Only use it when you notice visible image retention.

title: HDMI HDCP Handshake Failure
shortDescription: The TV cannot complete the HDCP copy protection handshake with a connected device, causing a black or purple screen.
fullDescription:
HDMI HDCP (High-bandwidth Digital Content Protection) handshake failures occur when the TV and a connected device cannot negotiate the copy protection protocol required to display content. This manifests as a black screen, purple/pink screen, or a message about HDCP not being supported.

HDCP handshake failures are common when using HDMI splitters, AV receivers, capture cards, or older devices that only support HDCP 1.4 while the content requires HDCP 2.2 or 2.3. Streaming sticks and 4K content are particularly prone to HDCP issues.

The HDCP handshake must succeed across the entire chain — from the source device, through any intermediate devices (AV receivers, splitters), to the TV. A failure at any point in the chain prevents the content from being displayed.

causes.cause: HDMI splitter or switch not HDCP compliant
HDMI cable not supporting required HDCP version
Source device HDCP version lower than required
TV HDMI port HDCP controller malfunction

symptoms: Black screen when playing content on HDMI device
Purple or pink tinted screen instead of video
HDCP error message on screen
Content plays for a few seconds then goes black
4K content fails but 1080p content works
Screen flashes intermittently

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the HDMI chain
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug the TV and all connected HDMI devices (AV receiver, streaming stick, cable box). Wait 30 seconds. Plug in and power on the TV first, then the source device. This forces a fresh HDCP handshake.

fixSteps.title: Connect directly to the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Remove any HDMI splitters, switches, or AV receivers from the chain. Connect the source device directly to the TV with a single HDMI cable. If this works, the intermediate device is causing the HDCP failure.

fixSteps.title: Enable HDMI Deep Color
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Devices > HDMI Settings > HDMI Deep Color. Enable it for the port your device is connected to. HDCP 2.2 requires Deep Color to be enabled for 4K HDR content.

fixSteps.title: Try a different HDMI cable
fixSteps.instruction: Use a Premium High Speed HDMI cable or Ultra High Speed HDMI cable. Older or low-quality cables may not reliably pass HDCP signals. For 4K content, an HDMI 2.0 or 2.1 rated cable is required.

fixSteps.title: Update source device firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Check for firmware updates on your streaming device, cable box, or game console. HDCP issues can be caused by bugs in the source device's HDMI output that are fixed in newer firmware.

whenToCallPro: If HDCP handshake fails on all HDMI ports with multiple different devices and cables, the TV's HDCP controller on the main board may be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV HDCP handshake failure black screen fix

faq.q: What is HDCP and why does it cause problems?
faq.a: HDCP is a copy protection system built into HDMI. Every device in the HDMI chain must support the same HDCP version. If any device fails the handshake, the content is blocked. This is a security feature, not a TV defect.

faq.q: Why does HDCP fail only on certain content?
faq.a: 4K and HDR content requires HDCP 2.2 or higher, while older 1080p content only needs HDCP 1.4. If your HDMI chain only supports HDCP 1.4, 4K content will fail while 1080p continues to work.

title: TV Turns Off Randomly or Unexpectedly
shortDescription: The TV shuts off on its own without user input, either immediately or after a period of use.
fullDescription:
LG TVs turning off randomly is a common complaint that can be caused by several built-in features or hardware issues. LG TVs have multiple automatic power-off features: a sleep timer, an auto power off timer (turns off after 4 hours of no input), an eco mode that turns off when no signal is detected, and SimpLink/CEC which can turn the TV off when a connected device powers down.

Before assuming a hardware fault, these automatic features should be checked and disabled if they are causing unwanted shutdowns. The most common culprit is the 'Auto Power Off' timer that is enabled by default and shuts the TV off after 4 hours if no remote buttons are pressed.

If the TV turns off and immediately back on (power cycling), or turns off very shortly after being powered on (within seconds), the issue is more likely a failing power supply board or overheating.

causes.cause: Auto Power Off timer is enabled (4-hour default)
SimpLink/CEC turning TV off with another device
Eco Mode turning TV off when no signal detected

symptoms: TV turns off after approximately 4 hours
TV turns off at seemingly random intervals
TV turns off when another device is powered down
TV turns off shortly after being turned on
TV restarts on its own repeatedly
TV turns off when switching inputs

fixSteps.title: Disable Auto Power Off
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Timers > Auto Power Off (or Settings > General > Energy Saving > Auto Power Off). Set it to OFF. This feature turns the TV off after 4 hours of no remote activity by default.

fixSteps.title: Check and disable Sleep Timer
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Timers > Sleep Timer. Make sure it is set to OFF. If a sleep timer is set, the TV will turn off after the specified duration.

fixSteps.title: Disable SimpLink Auto Power Sync
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Devices > HDMI Settings > SimpLink > Auto Power Sync. Turn it OFF. When enabled, the TV may turn off when a CEC-connected device powers down.

fixSteps.title: Turn off No Signal Power Off
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Energy Saving (or Power Saving). Disable 'No Signal Power Off' or 'Auto Off When No Signal.' This feature turns the TV off when no input signal is detected for a set period.

fixSteps.title: Check for overheating
fixSteps.instruction: Feel the back of the TV. If it is very hot, ensure adequate ventilation (4+ inches clearance). Clean any dust from the vents using compressed air. Overheating triggers a thermal protection shutdown.

whenToCallPro: If the TV turns off within seconds of being powered on despite all timers being disabled and the TV not being hot, the power supply board is likely failing and needs professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV turns off by itself fix auto power off

faq.q: Why does my LG TV turn off after exactly 4 hours?
faq.a: This is the Auto Power Off feature, which is enabled by default on LG TVs. It turns the TV off after 4 hours if no remote button is pressed, even if you are actively watching. Go to Settings > General > Timers > Auto Power Off and set it to OFF.

faq.q: My LG TV turns off every time I turn off my PS5. Why?
faq.a: This is SimpLink (HDMI-CEC) Auto Power Sync. When your PS5 sends a CEC power-off signal, the TV follows. Disable Auto Power Sync in Settings > General > Devices > HDMI Settings > SimpLink.

title: OLED Image Retention or Burn-In
shortDescription: A faint ghost image of previously displayed static content remains visible on the OLED screen.
fullDescription:
Image retention on LG OLED TVs occurs when a static image (such as a channel logo, game HUD, or news ticker) is displayed for an extended period, leaving a faint permanent or semi-permanent impression on the screen. Temporary image retention usually fades on its own or after running the pixel refresher. Permanent burn-in occurs when the organic compounds in the OLED pixels degrade unevenly.

LG OLED TVs have several built-in protections against burn-in: the automatic pixel refresher, screen shift, logo luminance adjustment, and screen saver. Despite these protections, burn-in can still occur with prolonged static content display, especially at high brightness settings.

Temporary image retention is normal and not a defect — it typically disappears after displaying varied content or running the pixel refresher. Permanent burn-in, where the ghost image persists even after pixel refreshing, may be covered under LG's OLED warranty for panel replacement.

causes.cause: Extended display of static content (logos, HUDs)
High brightness settings accelerating pixel wear
Screen shift and pixel refresher disabled
Normal OLED panel aging with uneven usage
Defective OLED panel with premature degradation

symptoms: Faint ghost image visible on solid color backgrounds
Channel logo outline visible when watching different content
Game HUD elements visible after switching to other content
News ticker or scoreboard outline permanently visible
Uneven brightness across the screen on gray backgrounds

fixSteps.title: Display varied content
fixSteps.instruction: Play colorful, fast-moving content (like nature documentaries or colorful YouTube videos) for several hours. This exercises all pixels and can naturally reduce temporary image retention.

fixSteps.title: Run the manual pixel refresher
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > OLED Panel Care > Pixel Refresher > Start Now. The TV will turn off and run a compensation cycle for about 1 hour. This process is designed to reduce image retention.

fixSteps.title: Enable all panel care features
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > OLED Panel Care. Enable Screen Shift, Logo Luminance Adjustment, and Screen Saver. Set the screen saver to activate after 2 minutes of no input. These prevent future retention.

fixSteps.title: Reduce brightness and OLED light
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Picture > OLED Light (or Brightness) and reduce it to 50-70. High OLED light settings accelerate pixel wear. Also disable Energy Saving modes that force peak brightness.

fixSteps.title: Contact LG for warranty coverage
fixSteps.instruction: If permanent burn-in is confirmed (ghost image persists after multiple pixel refresher cycles), contact LG customer support. LG covers permanent OLED burn-in under warranty for some models and usage scenarios.

whenToCallPro: If permanent burn-in is visible and the pixel refresher does not resolve it. OLED panel replacement is the only fix for permanent burn-in and should be done under warranty if possible.
videoSearchQuery: LG OLED TV burn-in fix image retention

faq.q: Is image retention the same as burn-in?
faq.a: No. Image retention is temporary and fades on its own or with the pixel refresher. Burn-in is permanent damage to the OLED pixels. Temporary retention is normal and not a defect.

faq.q: Does LG warranty cover burn-in?
faq.a: LG has extended OLED burn-in coverage for some models and years. Contact LG support with your model number and serial number to check if your TV is covered.

faq.q: How can I prevent OLED burn-in?
faq.a: Vary your content, keep OLED brightness at a reasonable level, enable screen shift and logo dimming, use the screen saver, and avoid displaying static content for more than 30 minutes continuously.

title: Half Screen Dark or Lines on Display
shortDescription: Half of the TV screen is dark, or horizontal/vertical lines appear across the display.
fullDescription:
When half of an LG TV screen goes dark or displays horizontal/vertical lines, it typically indicates a hardware failure in the T-Con (timing controller) board, a loose or damaged ribbon cable between the T-Con board and the display panel, or in some cases, a failing display panel.

The T-Con board is responsible for translating the video signal from the main board into the precise timing signals that control each pixel row and column on the display. When one of its output channels fails or a ribbon cable loses contact, half the screen or specific regions lose their signal.
