Loose ribbon cable connections are the most fixable cause of this issue. Reseating the ribbon cables between the T-Con board and the panel often resolves the problem without requiring any new parts.

causes.cause: Loose ribbon cable between T-Con and display panel
Damaged or corroded ribbon cable connector
Display panel COF (Chip on Film) failure

symptoms: Top or bottom half of screen is completely black
Left or right half of screen is dark
Horizontal lines across the screen
Vertical colored lines on the display
Screen flickers between normal and half-dark
Lines appear only on certain inputs

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV for 60 seconds and plug it back in. Software glitches can occasionally cause display anomalies that look like hardware failures but resolve with a restart.

fixSteps.title: Check on different inputs
fixSteps.instruction: Try multiple inputs (HDMI, Live TV, Home screen). If the lines or dark area appear on ALL inputs including the TV menu, the issue is internal to the TV. If only on one input, the source device may be faulty.

fixSteps.title: Reseat ribbon cables
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV. Remove the back panel screws and carefully remove the back cover. Locate the T-Con board (small board near the center with ribbon cables going to the panel). Gently release the locking tabs on the ribbon cable connectors and reseat the cables firmly.

fixSteps.title: Replace the T-Con board
fixSteps.instruction: If reseating cables does not help, the T-Con board may have a failed output. Order a replacement T-Con board matching your TV model. Disconnect the ribbon cables and power connector, remove the old board, and install the replacement.

fixSteps.title: Inspect ribbon cables for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Examine each ribbon cable closely for tears, creases, or burned/corroded contacts. Damaged cables cannot be repaired and the cable or entire panel assembly may need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If reseating cables and replacing the T-Con board does not fix the issue, the display panel itself may have a COF (Chip on Film) failure, which typically makes the TV uneconomical to repair.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV half screen dark lines fix T-Con

faq.q: Can a TV with half the screen dark be repaired?
faq.a: Often yes. If the cause is a loose ribbon cable or a failed T-Con board, the repair is relatively affordable ($25-70 for the board plus labor). If the display panel itself has failed, repair may not be cost-effective.

faq.q: Why did half my screen suddenly go dark?
faq.a: This usually happens when a ribbon cable connector loosens over time due to thermal expansion and contraction, or when the T-Con board develops a fault. It can also happen after a power surge.

title: Network Authentication Error
shortDescription: The TV fails to authenticate with the wireless network due to an incorrect password or security protocol issue.
fullDescription:
Error 300 on LG Smart TVs indicates a network authentication failure. This means the TV can detect the Wi-Fi network but cannot authenticate — either the password is incorrect, the router's security protocol is incompatible, or MAC address filtering is blocking the TV.

This error is different from Error 106 (general connection failure) as it specifically relates to the authentication phase. It commonly occurs after changing the Wi-Fi password, after a router firmware update that changes security settings, or when connecting to a new network.

LG TVs support WPA2 and WPA3 security protocols. Some older models may have issues with WPA3-only networks or certain enterprise authentication methods (WPA2-Enterprise).

causes.cause: Router security protocol incompatible with TV
MAC address filtering blocking the TV

symptoms: Error 300 when attempting to connect to Wi-Fi
TV detects the network but cannot join
Authentication failed message after entering password
TV was previously connected but now cannot authenticate

fixSteps.title: Re-enter Wi-Fi password carefully
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network > Wi-Fi Connection. Select your network, forget it if previously saved, then reconnect. Enter the password character by character, paying attention to uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and special characters.

fixSteps.title: Verify password on another device
fixSteps.instruction: Try connecting a phone or laptop to the same Wi-Fi network with the same password. If the password works on another device but not the TV, the issue may be with security protocol compatibility.

fixSteps.title: Check router security settings
fixSteps.instruction: Log into your router admin panel. Set the wireless security to WPA2-Personal (WPA2-PSK) with AES encryption. Some LG TVs have issues with WPA3, TKIP encryption, or mixed WPA/WPA2 modes.

fixSteps.title: Disable MAC filtering
fixSteps.instruction: In your router's admin panel, check if MAC address filtering is enabled. If it is, either disable it or add your TV's MAC address to the allowed list. Find your TV's MAC address in Settings > Network > Wi-Fi Connection > Advanced Settings.

fixSteps.title: Reset TV network settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network > Reset Network Settings. This clears all saved Wi-Fi networks and credentials. Reconnect to your Wi-Fi network with the correct password.

whenToCallPro: This error does not require professional TV repair. If you cannot resolve the router security compatibility, contact your ISP or a network technician for router configuration help.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV error 300 WiFi authentication fix

faq.q: Why does Error 300 appear after I changed my Wi-Fi password?
faq.a: The TV is still trying to connect with the old password stored in its memory. Go to Settings > Network, forget the old network connection, and reconnect with the new password.

faq.q: Can my LG TV connect to WPA3 networks?
faq.a: Newer LG TVs (2020+) support WPA3. Older models may only support WPA2. If your router is set to WPA3-only, try switching it to WPA2/WPA3 mixed mode for compatibility.

displayCode: This App Is No Longer Supported
title: App No Longer Supported on WebOS Version
shortDescription: A streaming app is no longer available or supported on the TV's version of WebOS.
fullDescription:
As streaming services update their apps, older versions of LG WebOS may lose support. When this happens, the app either disappears from the LG Content Store, fails to launch with a 'no longer supported' message, or works but with degraded functionality.

This is particularly common on LG TVs from 2016 or earlier, as app developers drop support for older WebOS versions (1.0, 2.0, 3.0). Even some 2017-2018 models running WebOS 3.5 and 4.0 are beginning to lose support for certain apps.

There is no way to force an unsupported app to work on an incompatible WebOS version. The recommended solution is to use an external streaming device (Roku, Fire TV Stick, Chromecast, Apple TV) connected via HDMI, which provides its own app ecosystem independent of the TV's software.

causes.cause: WebOS version too old for current app version
App developer discontinued support for the platform
TV region does not have the app available
App removed from LG Content Store

symptoms: App shows 'no longer supported' message
App disappeared from the Content Store
App crashes immediately when opened
App loads but shows incompatibility warning
App works but some features are missing or broken

fixSteps.title: Update TV firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > About This TV > Check for Updates. Install any available firmware update. Sometimes a firmware update restores app compatibility or includes an updated version of the app.

fixSteps.title: Reinstall the app
fixSteps.instruction: Delete the app from the TV: press and hold on the app icon, select Remove. Go to the LG Content Store and search for the app. If it is still available, reinstall it. The fresh install may include the latest compatible version.

fixSteps.title: Use screen casting
fixSteps.instruction: If the app is no longer available on the TV, cast content from your phone instead. Open the app on your phone and use the cast/mirror button. LG TVs support AirPlay (2019+ models), Miracast, and the LG ThinQ app for screen sharing.

fixSteps.title: Use an external streaming device
fixSteps.instruction: Purchase a streaming device like a Roku Stick, Amazon Fire TV Stick, Chromecast with Google TV, or Apple TV. Connect it to an HDMI port on the TV. These devices have their own app stores and receive updates independently of the TV.

fixSteps.title: Use the TV's web browser
fixSteps.instruction: As a last resort, some streaming services can be accessed through the TV's built-in web browser. Press Home, open the Web Browser app, and navigate to the service's website. Note that some services block browser access on TVs.

whenToCallPro: This issue cannot be fixed by a professional repair technician. The TV's hardware is fine — the software ecosystem has simply moved past the TV's capabilities. An external streaming device is the solution.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV app not supported fix streaming device

faq.q: Why did my app stop working on my LG TV?
faq.a: App developers periodically drop support for older TV platforms. This is similar to how phone apps stop supporting old phone models. The TV hardware is fine, but the software environment is no longer maintained for your WebOS version.

faq.q: Which streaming device is best for an older LG TV?
faq.a: The Amazon Fire TV Stick 4K and Roku Streaming Stick 4K are popular budget options ($25-50). The Chromecast with Google TV and Apple TV 4K are premium options with more features. All connect via HDMI and work with any TV.

faq.q: Will LG ever update my old TV to support new apps?
faq.a: LG typically provides WebOS updates for 3-4 years after a TV model's release. After that, the TV continues to work but does not receive new WebOS versions, so app compatibility gradually decreases.

title: Screen Flickering or Flashing
shortDescription: The TV screen flickers, flashes, or rapidly alternates between bright and dark during operation.
fullDescription:
Screen flickering on LG TVs can range from a subtle rapid brightness variation to intense flashing that makes the TV unwatchable. The causes vary widely depending on whether the flickering affects the entire screen or specific areas, and whether it occurs on all inputs or only certain ones.

Common software causes include the Energy Saving mode rapidly adjusting brightness based on content, TruMotion settings causing judder, or a refresh rate mismatch with the source. Hardware causes include a failing LED backlight driver, loose internal cable connections, or a power supply that cannot maintain stable voltage.

If the flickering only occurs with specific content or inputs, it is likely a settings or source issue. If it occurs on all inputs including the TV's own menus, it is more likely a hardware issue.

causes.cause: Energy Saving mode rapidly adjusting backlight
TruMotion or motion smoothing causing artifacts
Refresh rate mismatch with source device

symptoms: Screen rapidly alternates between bright and dim
Flickering only on certain channels or apps
Screen flashes black momentarily during viewing
Backlight visibly pulses or strobes
Flickering worsens over time
Flickering only occurs when TV is cold/warm

fixSteps.title: Disable Energy Saving mode
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Energy Saving. Set it to OFF or Minimum. The Auto or Maximum settings aggressively adjust backlight brightness frame-by-frame, which appears as flickering.

fixSteps.title: Turn off TruMotion
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Picture > Advanced Settings > Clarity > TruMotion. Set it to OFF. Motion processing can cause flickering or judder artifacts, especially with film content.

fixSteps.title: Disable Instant Game Response
fixSteps.instruction: If flickering occurs during gaming, go to Settings > General > Devices > HDMI Settings > Instant Game Response. Turn it OFF. Some games combined with VRR (Variable Refresh Rate) can cause visible flickering.

fixSteps.title: Try different picture mode
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Picture > Picture Mode. Switch to a different mode (Standard, Cinema, Game). Some picture modes have settings that can cause flickering. Test in Standard mode with all processing turned off.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle and check connections
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV for 5 minutes. If comfortable, remove the back panel and check that all internal ribbon cables and power connectors are firmly seated. Vibration from speakers can gradually loosen connections.

whenToCallPro: If flickering occurs on all inputs including the TV's own menu system after disabling Energy Saving and all picture processing, the backlight driver or power supply board may be failing.
videoSearchQuery: LG TV screen flickering fix

faq.q: Why does my LG TV flicker only at night?
faq.a: The Energy Saving mode uses an ambient light sensor that becomes more aggressive in darker environments. Disable Energy Saving in Settings > General > Energy Saving to stop the automatic brightness adjustments.

faq.q: Is OLED flickering near black different from LED flickering?
faq.a: Yes. LG OLED TVs may exhibit 'near-black flickering' where very dark scenes appear to pulse. This is related to the ABL (Auto Brightness Limiter) and can sometimes be reduced by changing the picture mode to Filmmaker or Cinema.
